title: Field Types and Options in Django Models

content.problemStatement:
In Django models, field types and options define the structure and behavior of your database tables. You must have already seen some of the field types that we used in the previous examples/ problems as we dealt with models. \
In this lesson we will go through the various field types and options available in Django models.

#### Field Types:
Some common field types provided by Django include:

1. CharField: For storing short strings (e.g., names, titles)
2. TextField: For longer text content
3. IntegerField: For storing whole numbers
4. FloatField: For decimal numbers
5. BooleanField: For true/false values
6. DateTimeField: For date and time information
7. ForeignKey: For creating relationships between models (as we learned in Many-to-One relationships)

#### Field Options:
Field options allow you to customize the behavior of your fields. Some common options include:

1. null: Allows the field to store NULL values in the database
2. blank: Allows the field to be left empty in forms
3. default: Sets a default value for the field
4. unique: Ensures that each value in this field is unique across the table
5. verbose_name: A human-readable name for the field (used in the admin interface)

We will see  how to use field types and options in a models in the following examples.
